extra.reasoning: The property "globally(opted_out implies not sent)" is VIOLATED. Transition t40 (on send_attempt if opted_out and stage >= 12: sent := true) sets sent true while opted_out is true. This is reachable because optout (t1) only modifies opted_out and sent, never stage, so the stage can be advanced to 12 first and then preserved across an optout. Path: the 12-event unlock sequence advancing stage 0->12 is key_1 (t3, 0->1), key_2 (t6, 1->2), key_2 (t9, 2->3), key_2 (t12, 3->4), key_0 (t15, 4->5), key_2 (t18, 5->6), key_1 (t21, 6->7), key_0 (t24, 7->8), key_1 (t27, 8->9), key_1 (t30, 9->10), key_2 (t33, 10->11), key_0 (t36, 11->12). Then optout (t1) sets opted_out=true, sent=false, stage stays 12. Then send_attempt: t2 is disabled (opted_out is true), t39 is disabled (stage is not < 12), so t40 fires, setting sent=true. Final state opted_out=true, sent=true, stage=12 violates the invariant. The trace is 14 events, within the 15-event bound. Verified by direct simulation.
